title: LCROSS Impactor Data Bundle
description: This bundle contains all data collected from instruments onboard the LCROSS Shepherding Spacecraft during its four month mission. Because the purpose of this mission was to observe the impact of a centaur launch stage in a permanently shadowed region of the moon, the most interesting data generated by LCROSS was received just before and after that impact. The bundle contains both raw, partially processed, and calibrated data products.
missions: Lunar Crater Observation and Sensing Satellite
instrument_hosts: Lunar Crater Observation and Sensing Satellite

The data were used to determine the gravity field and shape of Mercury and to improve solar system ephemerides. The bundle is a migration of data from the original PDS3 archive with minor improvements and corrections.

title: Nathair Facula Topography and Photometric Cube Data V1.0
description: This bundle contains regional topography and photometric data for two locations within Nathair Facula, Mercury. Both locations focus on volcanic vents and pyroclastic deposits near 35.86 Lat 63.93 E Lon. The data was generated using the Gaskell Stereophotoclinometry (SPC) software suite with images from the MESSENGER mission to Mercury. In addition to the topographic heights of the Digital Terrain Model (DTM), photometric data is aligned to and has the same horizontal spacing as the topographic data. For select Mercury Dual Imaging System (MDIS) Narrow Angle Camera (NAC) or Wide Angle Camera (WAC) images, four photometric data products are available; I/F (reflectance), Local Phase Angle, Local Emission Angle, and Local Incidence Angle. One DTM represents heights every 120 m and has a size of 130 by 130 km, while the other represents heights every 60 m and has a size of 80 by 80 km. The two DTMs partially overlap.

title: Kaguya Gamma-Ray Spectrometer Corrected Spectra Bundle
description: This archive contains Kaguya Gamma-Ray Spectrometer (KGRS) corrected and calibrated time-series spectra and ephemerides, pointing, and geometry (EPG) data for the nominal and extended observation periods of JAXA's SELENE (Kaguya) mission. The SELENE mission was the Japanese mission to the Moon with a main orbiter "Kaguya" and two daughter relay satellites launched on Sep. 14, 2007. KGRS was mounted on the deck of the main orbiter. It employed a high-purity Ge (HPGe) crystal as a main detector, and surrounding BGO and plastic scintillators as anti-coincidence detectors to reduce backgrounds. The Ge crystal was cooled by a Sterling refrigerator. It made close-proximity observations of the Moon from Dec. 14, 2007 to Dec. 11, 2008 at the high circular orbital altitude of 100 km, and from Feb. 10 to May 28 in 2009 at the low elliptical orbital altitude of ~30 x 50 km. KGRS recorded energy spectra of gamma rays in two gain modes simultaneously; high gain (0.1 to 3 MeV) and low gain (0.1 to 13 MeV), each of which was analyzed by the 13-bit (8192 ch) analog-to-digital converter (ADC). While the former has a higher spectral resolution over the energy range of radioactive elements, the latter has better statistics and covers the whole energy range of major rock-forming elements, including the Fe neutron capture line at 7.6 MeV. The accumulation time of each spectrum was 17 s. Both modes of spectra are included in this archive. The observation epochs of KGRS consists of three regular measurement periods (Period 1 to 3), background measurements, and the annealing of the Ge crystal. The HPGe detector was annealed from 16 to 25, December 2008, which improved energy resolution by a factor of two. Period 1 and 2 lasted two and five months, respectively, with limited spatial or spectral resolutions. Period 3 lasted ~3.5 months (including dead time) with the best spatial and spectral resolutions. In addition, spectra from the background measurement when the satellite was flipped and the detector faced deep space can be analyzed to estimate the background gamma-ray intensity from the satellite due to the exposure to galactic cosmic rays. This archive consists of three spectrum files (each corresponding to one KGRS epoch) and one unified EPG file. The spectrum files contain a time series of corrected and calibrated pulse height spectra acquired by KGRS's HPGe detector in the lunar orbit. In the spectrum files, each row contains the spacecraft clock (sclk) ticks and UTC time corresponding to the end of the accumulation interval (17 s), with the two sets of the 8192-channel HPGe spectra accumulated. In the EPG file, each row starts with sclk recorded at the end of the accumulation interval to match spacecraft positions, pointing, and correction factors with the corrected spectra. Note that the UTC time and ephemeris time included in the EPG file correspond to the midpoint of the accumulation interval. The Kaguya Gamma-Ray Spectrometer Corrected Spectra bundle was created by Naoyuki Yamashita at Planetary Science Institute, AZ, USA, with the permission of JAXA and the KGRS instrument PI Prof. Nobuyuki Hasebe. This work was supported by the NASA under Grant No. NNX16AG54G issued through the Planetary Data Archiving, Restoration, and Tools Program (PDART), Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Sciences (ROSES) 2015.

title: Apollo 15 Lunar Surface Experiments Package Heat Flow Experiment Data Bundle
description: This data set comprises a reduced, subsampled set of the data returned from the Apollo 15 Heat Flow Experiment from 31 July 1971 through 31 December 1974. The experiment consisted of two probes placed by the Apollo 15 astronauts in holes drilled in the lunar surface near the Apollo 15 Lunar Surface Experiments Package site to measure the thermal conductivity. The data consist of a set of ten ASCII tables with time, temperature differences, and average temperatures readings measured by the thermocouples in the heat flow probes and probe cables. The data have been restored and reformatted from binary data held on magnetic tapes at the NASA National Space Science Data Center (NSSDC) under NSSDC ID of PSPG-00093 (old NSSDC ID 71-063C-06A).
instruments: Apollo 15 Lunar Surface Experiments Package Heat Flow Experiment

Supporting documentation includes digitized scans in JPEG and PDF format of the original LSRP soil mechanics dataset on microfilm, videos and transcripts of the LSRP as it was operated on the Moon, diagrams and maps of the experiment areas, and photography of the Apollo 15 Station 8 where the experiment was performed.
instruments: Apollo 15 Lunar Module Lunar Self-Recording Penetrometer

title: Apollo 17 Lunar Surface Experiments Package Heat Flow Experiment ASCII Data Bundle
description: This data set comprises a reduced, subsampled set of the data returned from the Apollo 17 Heat Flow Experiment from 12 December 1972 through 31 December 1974. The experiment consisted of two probes placed by the Apollo 17 astronauts in holes drilled in the lunar surface near the Apollo 17 Lunar Surface Experiments Package site to measure the thermal conductivity. The data consist of a set of ten ASCII tables with time, temperature differences, and average temperatures readings measured by the thermocouples in the heat flow probes and probe cables. The data have been restored and reformatted from binary data held on magnetic tapes at the NASA National Space Science Data Center (NSSDC) under NSSDC ID of PSPG-00022 (old NSSDC ID 72-096C-01A).
instruments: Apollo 17 Lunar Surface Experiments Package Heat Flow Experiment

title: Apollo 17 Lunar Traverse Gravimeter Data Bundle
description: This data set contains a table of readings from the Traverse Gravimeter Experiment performed during the Apollo 17 mission. The gravimeter, which recorded relative measurements of the local gravitational field at the lunar surface, was manually run by the astronauts near the Lunar Module landing site. The data table includes 23 valid gravity values, with calibrations and corrections, that were obtained from 12 to 14 December 1972. Supporting documentation includes the Apollo 17 Traverse Gravimeter Experiment Final Report.

title: Radio Science Documentation Bundle
description: This bundle contains documentation about data products that are collected using radio science and supporting equipment. With one exception, each member collection contains one or more versions of a single Software Interface Specification (SIS) or an equivalent document. A SIS describes the format and content of a data file at a granularity suffient for use -- typically byte-level, but sometimes bit-level. Examples of products and descriptions of their use may also be included in a collection, as appropriate. The exception is the DOCUMENT collection, which contains supporting material -- usually journal publications, technical reports, or other documents that describe investigations, analysis methods, and/or data but not at the level of a SIS. Members of the DOCUMENT collection were usually released once, whereas a SIS often evolves over many years.

title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD 3D Radargram Bundle
description: This bundle contains three-dimensional (3D) volumes of radar sounding data for Mars collected by the SHARAD instrument on the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter. Each such 3D radargram consists of an amalgamation of data collected over many SHARAD tracks crossing a given projected area. Each 3D radargram contains values of the radar backscatter power, strength, or amplitude arranged in an array with either time delay or depth on the fastest varying (Z) axis, the projection X coordinate on the slowest varying axis, and the projection Y coordinate on the intermediately varying axis. The label file for each 3D radargram contains information about the axes sampling intervals, projections, and data scaling.

title: Apollo 14 CPLEE Electron and Ion Count Rates and Fluxes Bundle
description: This bundle contains fixed-width ASCII tables of raw counts and derived electron and ion fluxes (40-20,000 eV) from the Apollo 14 ALSEP Charged Particle Lunar Environment Experiment (CPLEE). Two analyzers observed particles: one vertically oriented and one at 60 degrees toward lunar west. Data were acquired via automatic 19.2-second voltage stepping (15-channel spectrum) and manual 2.4-second sampling modes. The dataset includes housekeeping measurements and the necessary time and mode identification information. Coverage spans 05 February 1971 through 02 March 1973, including the major solar flare of 04 August 1972.
instruments: Apollo 14 Lunar Surface Experiments Package Charged Particle Lunar Environment Experiment
instrument_hosts: The Apollo 14 Lunar Surface Experiments Package

title: Mars Odyssey GRS Special Bundle
description: Mars Odyssey GRS Special Products. The Odyssey Gamma Ray Spectrometer (GRS) special data products are derived data acquired by the Gamma Ray Subsystem -- the Gamma Sensor Head (GSH), the Neutron Spectrometer (NS), and the High Energy Neutron Detector (HEND). The Odyssey GRS Science Team released this set of special derived products to accompany the October 1, 2002, release of EDRs (raw data). These products include global maps of neutron flux and portions of gamma-ray spectra showing hydrogen peaks. Figures in Science articles published in 2002 by the GRS Team were based on these data.

title: Chandrayaan-1 Mini-RF Data Bundle

title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.

title: Apollo 16 Metric (Mapping) and Panoramic Cameras - Photographic Ephemeris Support Data Collection
description: This collection contains comma-separated ASCII tables of ephemeris data (spacecraft state vectors, camera orientation, photograph position, and lighting data) computed during the Apollo era for analyzing and using photographic images taken of the moon on 20-24 April 1972 by the Apollo 16 Metric (Mapping) and Panoramic Cameras from lunar orbit. These tables were produced using optical character recognition (OCR) software to read TIFF images of scanned tabular output on microfilm. These scans were merged and provided in this collection as two PDF/A files, one for each camera.

title: Apollo 17 Far-Ultraviolet Spectrometer (FUVS) Data Graphs Collection
description: This collection contains graphs of some of the spectral data acquired by the Apollo 17 Far-Ultraviolet Spectrometer from lunar orbit during 10-19 December 1972. The spectral data graphs, which were scanned from microfilm and provided in high-resolution PDF/A format, are displayed in two forms: averages of the spectra from 118.4 to 167.2 nanometers (1184.00 to 1671.57 Angstroms); and, time variations in the intensities observed at wavelengths of 121.567 or 121.570, 123.6 or 127.5, 130.40, 147.0, 152.0 or 155.0 or 155.600, and 165.700 nanometers (1215.67 or 1215.70, 1236.00 or 1275.00, 1304.00, 1470.00, 1520.00 or 1550.00 or 1556.00, and 1657.00 Angstroms).

title: Apollo 12 ALSEP ARCSAV SIDE/CCIG Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-093 to 1975-182)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science and engineering measurements acquired by the Suprathermal Ion Detector Experiment (SIDE) and the Cold Cathode Ion Gage Experiment (CCIG; also known as the Cold Cathode Gage Experiment for the time span of 03 April through 01 July 1975. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious tape read errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes one ASCII file of SIDE/CCIG-related command verification data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.
instruments: Apollo 12 Cold Cathode Ion Gage Experiment

title: Apollo 12 ALSEP ARCSAV SIDE/CCIG Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1975-093 to 1975-182)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 12 ALSEP SIDE/CCIG Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-093 to 1975-182). It includes a description of the raw data products and the Fortran programs used to extract data from raw binary Apollo 12 SIDE/CCIG ARCSAV files and transform to ASCII. It also includes a one-page sheet of SIDE calibration data.
instruments: Apollo 12 Cold Cathode Ion Gage Experiment

title: Apollo 12 ALSEP ARCSAV Solar Wind Spectrometer Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-093 to 1975-182)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science measurements and daily raw calibration and engineering data acquired by the Solar Wind Spectrometer (SWS) at the Apollo 12 landing site for the time span of 03 April through 01 July 1975. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious tape read errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes one ASCII file of SWS-related command verification data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.

title: Apollo 12 ALSEP ARCSAV Solar Wind Spectrometer Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1975-093 to 1975-182)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 12 ALSEP ARCSAV Solar Wind Spectrometer Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-093 to 1975-182). It includes a description of the raw data products and the Fortran programs used to extract data from raw binary Apollo 12 SWS ARCSAV files and transform to ASCII.

title: Apollo 15 and 17 Heat Flow Experiment Concatenated Data Sets: Data Collection
description: This collection contains ASCII tables containing corrected, reduced, and concatenated versions of data from all available sources for the Apollo 15 and 17 Heat Flow Experiment. Subdirectories contain three separate reduced sets: 'clean,' 'split,' and 'depth.' These tables are based on other data in the PDS and the published literature, specifically (1) transcriptions of data sent by the original instrument team to the NSSDC and (2) data not archived by the instrument team and recovered much later from ARCSAV tapes. The data here correct several errors in (1), and furthermore place (1) and (2) into a standardized format for ease of use.

title: Apollo 15 and 17 Heat Flow Experiment Concatenated Data Sets: Documentation Collection
description: This collection includes (1) direct user-facing documentation for urn:nasa:pds:a15_17_hfe_concatenated:data and (2) documents that provide useful context for the HFE experiment and the bundle's source data.

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Concatenated Data Sets: Source Materials Collection
description: This collection contains source data and reduction code for the reduced, corrected, and concatenated sets in this bundle's data collection. Most of these source data are available elsewhere on the PDS; the remainder are available in published scientific literature. They are copied in this bundle in order to (1) provide a 'snapshot' of the source material for our reduction process, should those archives change; and (2) to facilitate bug hunting, verification, and functionality-adding software forks. This is a document collection because it does not contain the primary science data of the bundle and includes data that is not in a PDS4 observational product format.

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Calibrated Gradient Bridge Temperatures Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files contains fully processed, calibrated temperature data for the upper and lower gradient bridges (thermal sensors) on both probes of the Heat Flow Experiment (HFE) at the Apollo 15 landing site. The time span is 02 April through 30 June 1975. These data were derived from raw measurements extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes.

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Calibrated Gradient Bridge Temperatures Document Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Calibrated Gradient Bridge Temperatures Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181). It includes a description of the calibrated data products.

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains two fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science measurements acquired by the Heat Flow Experiment (HFE) at the Apollo 15 landing site for the time span of 02 April through 30 June 1975. One table provides Mode 1 (Gradient) data while the other table provides Mode 3 (Housekeeping) data. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious tape read errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes ASCII files of HFE-related command verification and raw analog housekeeping data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181). It includes a description of the raw data products and the Fortran programs used to extract data from raw binary Apollo 15 HFE ARCSAV files and transform to ASCII.

title: Apollo 15 Orbital Mass Spectrometer Data Output Scans Collection
description: This collection contains microfilm scans of formatted outputs of all data acquired by the Apollo 15 Orbital Mass Spectrometer from lunar orbit during 30 July to 07 August 1971, along with relevant documentation. The two mass ranges covered are 12-28 and 28-67 atomic mass units. The outputs include formatted summaries of peak amplitudes, trajectory data, and housekeeping measurements. The scans are provided as multipage PDF/A files.

title: Apollo 15 Metric (Mapping) and Panoramic Cameras - Photographic Ephemeris Support Data Collection
description: This collection contains comma-separated ASCII tables of ephemeris data (spacecraft state vectors, camera orientation, photograph position, and lighting data) computed during the Apollo era for analyzing and using photographic images taken of the moon from 30 July to 4 August 1971 by the Apollo 15 Metric (Mapping) and Panoramic Cameras from lunar orbit. These tables were produced using optical character recognition (OCR) software to read TIFF images of scanned tabular output on microfilm. These scans were merged and provided in this collection as two PDF/A files, one for each camera.

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV SIDE/CCIG Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science and engineering measurements acquired by the Suprathermal Ion Detector Experiment (SIDE) and the Cold Cathode Ion Gage Experiment (CCIG; also known as the Cold Cathode Gage Experiment for the time span of 02 April through 30 June 1975. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious tape read errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes one ASCII file of SIDE/CCIG-related command verification data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.
instruments: Apollo 15 Cold Cathode Ion Gage Experiment

title: Apollo 15 ALSEP ARCSAV SIDE/CCIG Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 15 ALSEP SIDE/CCIG Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181). It includes a description of the raw data products and the Fortran programs used to extract data from raw binary Apollo 15 SIDE/CCIG ARCSAV files and transform to ASCII. It also includes a one-page sheet of SIDE calibration data.
instruments: Apollo 15 Cold Cathode Ion Gage Experiment

title: Apollo 16 ALSEP ARCSAV Lunar Surface Magnetometer Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science and engineering measurements acquired by the Lunar Surface Magnetometer (LSM) at the Apollo 16 landing site for the time span of 02 April through 30 June 1975. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious tape read errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes one ASCII file of LSM-related command verification data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.

title: Apollo 16 ALSEP ARCSAV Lunar Surface Magnetometer Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 16 ALSEP ARCSAV Lunar Surface Magnetometer Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181). It includes a description of the raw data products and the Fortran programs used to extract data from raw binary Apollo 16 LSM ARCSAV files and transform to ASCII.

title: Apollo 16 Orbital Mass Spectrometer Data Output Scans Collection
description: This collection contains microfilm scans of formatted outputs of all data acquired by the Apollo 16 Orbital Mass Spectrometer from lunar orbit during 20-24 April 1972, along with relevant documentation. The two mass ranges covered are 12-28 and 28-67 atomic mass units. The outputs include formatted summaries of peak amplitudes, trajectory data, and housekeeping measurements. The scans are provided as multipage PDF/A files.

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Calibrated Gradient Bridge Temperatures Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files contains fully processed, calibrated temperature data for the upper gradient bridge (thermal sensors) on both probes of the Heat Flow Experiment (HFE) at the Apollo 17 landing site. The time span is 02 April through 30 June 1975. These data were derived from raw measurements extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes.

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Calibrated Gradient Bridge Temperatures Document Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 17 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Calibrated Gradient Bridge Temperatures Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181). It includes a description of the calibrated data products.

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains two fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science measurements acquired by the Heat Flow Experiment (HFE) at the Apollo 17 landing site for the time span of 02 April through 30 June 1975. One table provides Mode 1 (Gradient) data while the other table provides Mode 3 (Housekeeping) data. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious tape read errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes ASCII files of HFE-related command verification and raw analog housekeeping data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP ARCSAV Heat Flow Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)

description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science measurements acquired by the Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites (LEAM) Experiment at the Apollo 17 landing site for the time span of 02 April through 30 June 1975. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. This collection also includes ASCII files of LEAM-related command verification and raw analog housekeeping data extracted from the ARCSAV binary files.
instruments: Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment

instruments: Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP Work Tape Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1976-061 to 1976-200)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science measurements acquired by the Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites (LEAM) Experiment at the Apollo 17 landing site for the time span of 01 March through 18 July 1976, which were the last 140 days of LEAM operation. These data were extracted from Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) normal-bit-rate Work Tape files that contain time-edited and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. The extracted binary data were corrected for obvious bit errors then transformed to ASCII format for this archive. This collection also includes ASCII files of LEAM-related command verification and raw analog housekeeping data extracted from the Work Tape binary files.
instruments: Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP Work Tape Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Document Collection (1976-061 to 1976-200)
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 17 ALSEP Work Tape Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1976-061 to 1976-200). includes a description of the raw data products, an explanation of the film ID numbering convention used in the raw science data products, and the Fortran programs used to extract data from raw binary Apollo 17 LEAM data from ALSEP normal-bit-rate Work Tape files and transform to ASCII.
instruments: Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment

title: Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment Calibration Notebook Collection
description: This collection contains a digital reproduction of one hand-written laboratory notebook of calibration analysis by Dr. Otto E. Berg for the Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites (LEAM) Experiment. Dr. Berg used this work along with analyses of data acquired by the Pioneer 8 and 9 Cosmic Dust Detectors, which were identical to LEAM detector, to calibrate the Apollo 17 LEAM data. Dr. Berg was the principal investigator for all three instruments.
instruments: Apollo 17 Lunar Ejecta And Meteorites Experiment

title: Apollo 17 ALSEP ARCSAV Lunar Surface Gravimeter Raw Cleaned ASCII Data Collection (1975-092 to 1975-181)
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII files of daily raw cleaned science measurements acquired by the Lunar Surface Gravimeter (LSG) at the Apollo 17 landing site for the time span of 02 April through 30 June 1975. These data were extracted from NASA's original Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) archive tapes, also known as ARCSAV tapes, that recorded 24-hour, time-edited, and rearranged raw binary data transmitted from the Moon. title: Apollo 17 Metric (Mapping) and Panoramic Cameras - Photographic Ephemeris Support Data Collection
description: This collection contains comma-separated ASCII tables of ephemeris data (spacecraft state vectors, camera orientation, photograph position, and lighting data) computed during the Apollo era for analyzing and using photographic images taken of the moon on 10-16 December 1972 by the Apollo 17 Metric (Mapping) and Panoramic Cameras from lunar orbit. These tables were produced using optical character recognition (OCR) software to read TIFF images of scanned tabular output on microfilm. These scans were merged and provided in this collection as two PDF/A files, one for each camera.

title: Apollo 17 Surface Electrical Properties Experiment Calibrated ASCII Data Collection
description: This collection two comma-delimited ASCII files of calibrated lunar surface electromagnetic field data at six measured frequencies from 1.0 to 32.1 MHz and navigation (range) data from the Surface Electrical Properties (SEP) Experiment at the Apollo 17 landing site for the time span of 11-13 December 1972. One file contains straightened science and navigation (range) data; the other file contains only unstraightened science data. These data were extracted from binary files held on magnetic tape at the NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive as data set PSPG-00559, then reformatted to ASCII. The bundle description in the document collection describes the format of these ASCII data products.

title: Apollo 17 Surface Electrical Properties Experiment Calibrated ASCII Data - Document Collection
description: This collection contains supporting documentation for the Apollo 17 Surface Electrical Properties Experiment Calibrated ASCII Data Bundle. It includes a bundle description document that describes the calibrated ASCII data products in the data collection and the contents of this document collection. This document collection includes a high-resolution digital reproduction (microfiche scans) of the final technical report for the Apollo 17 Surface Electrical Properties Experiment. The report contains plots of the complete set of processed lunar field data, as dB values versus range, at six measured frequencies from 1.0 to 32.1 MHz for the time span of 11-13 December 1972. The three-part report is provided as three PDF/A files.

title: Nathair Facula Topography and Photometric Cube Data V1.0
description: This bundle contains regional topography and photometric data for two locations within Nathair Facula, Mercury. Both locations focus on volcanic vents and pyroclastic deposits near 35 Lat 65 E Lon. The data was generated using the Gaskell Stereophotoclinometry (SPC) software suite with images from the MESSENGER mission to Mercury. In addition to the topographic heights of the Digital Terrain Model (DTM), photometric data is aligned to and has the same horizontal spacing as the topographic data. For select Mercury Dual Imaging System (MDIS) Narrow Angle Camera (NAC) or Wide Angle Camera (WAC) images, four photometric data products are available; I/F (reflectance), Local Phase Angle, Local Emission Angle, and Local Incidence Angle. One DTM represents heights every 120 m and has a size of 130 by 130 km, while the other represents heights every 60 m and has a size of 80 by 80 km. The two DTMs partially overlap.
instruments: MERCURY DUAL IMAGING SYSTEM NARROW ANGLE CAMERA
MERCURY DUAL IMAGING SYSTEM WIDE ANGLE CAMERA

title: Document collection for Arizona State University Ronald Greeley Center for Planetary Studies - The Ronald Greeley 35mm Slide Collection, The Jovian System, Callisto
description: This collection, included in document_callisto, consists of 129 digitized 35mm slides primarily of Callisto, Galilean Satellite of Jupiter, and other related bodies. Slides are divided into two databases, diagrams and photos, but are inventoried as a single collection. Information about each photograph, including location, description, and mission, are in the document_database in callisto_diagram_database.pdf and callisto_photo_database.pdf. The photographs and documentation are from the Dr. Ronald Greeley archive at the Arizona State University Ronald Greeley Center for Planetary Studies, Tempe, Arizona.

title: Arizona State University Ronald Greeley Center for Planetary Studies - The Ronald Greeley 35mm Slide Collection, The Jovian System

Information includes the location, subject matter, mission, and if the digitized slide was of a photograph or a diagram used for presentation/education purposes. Digitized images of the photographs are in the collections document_callisto, document_europa, document_ganymede, document_io, and document_jovian_system_and_misc also in this bundle. The digitized slides are identified whether they are a diagram or a photograph and are listed in separate descriptive databases (e.g., europa_diagram_database.pdf and europa_photo_database.pdf) but are inventoried in a single document (e.g., collection_document_europa_inventory.csv). The slides and information about them are from the Dr. Ronald Greeley archive at the Arizona State University Regional Planetary Image Facility, Tempe, Arizona.

title: Radio Science Documentation - Collection of Supporting Documents
description: This collection contains documents that support radio science but do not fall into the category of Software Interface Specification (SIS). A SIS describes the format and content of data files at the finest granularity required for use (typically byte, but sometimes bit). The documents in this collection are journal publications, technical reports, etc. that provide background about radio science investigations, analysis, and/or data collections but do not describe the data products in detail. In general, these documents were released only once, whereas a SIS often evolves over many years.

title: DSN Telecommunications Link Design Handbook
description: This collection includes several versions of the NASA Deep Space Network (DSN) Telecommunications Link Design Handbook. The Handbook provides information useful to flight projects contemplating the design of hardware and software, with reasonable assurance that the resulting telecommunications interfaces will be compatible with the established or planned DSN configurations. The Handbook is primarily concerned with performance parameters of equipment that supports the forward and return telecommunications link interfaces between spacecraft and the DSN. The Handbook consists of modules that present technical information applicable to the current DSN configuration and preliminary information applicable to future DSN configurations. The modules are revised to reflect new capabilities when these new capabilities have been planned and budgeted by the DSN Project Office.

title: Deep Space Network Service Catalog
description: This collection includes one or more versions of the NASA Deep Space Network (DSN) Services Catalog. Other supporting material may be included as appropriate. The Services Catalog provides an overview of the capabilities available from the DSN to support flight projects and experiment investigations. The capabilities described are focused on deep space missions, near-Earth missions above Geosynchronous Earth Orbit distance, and ground-based observational science, although many are potentially applicable to other mission domains. The descriptions are intended to aid those preparing mission and experiment proposals, as well as those in the early stages of project planning. This version of the document was in use through at least early 2020. Publication year of the collection is the publication year of the first version in the collection. Publication year of this collection is the publication year of the first version of the document in the collection.

title: REDR Collection
description: This collection contains one or more versions of the Radio Science Data Record (REDR) Software Interface Specification used when delivering open loop radio science data to investigators in the late 1970s. Publication year of this collection is the publication year of the first version in the collection. An example data file and a document explaining how to extract values from the binary format are also included in the collection.

title: MESSENGER MLA Navigation Assessment Document Collection

title: MESSENGER MLA Navigation Assessment SPICE Kernels Collection
description: The MESSENGER MLA Navigation Assessment SPICE Kernels collection consists of SPICE SPK files that were fragments of the navigation team's predicted orbit solution relevant to the time period over which MLA data were used to evaluate the spacecraft position at the given time. These orbit solutions were not modified by the MLA team. They are archived for reference only. The official, final reconstructed trajectory data archived at the NAIF PDS node in the data set MESS-E/V/H-SPICE-6-V1.0 should be used for all analysis.

description: The bsp collection contains the spacecraft and planetary ephemerides for 5 June 1994.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Catalog Collection
description: The catalog collection contains descriptive files for the Magellan mission, the spacecraft and ground support system, the radio instrumentation used to conduct the experiments, the data set itself (including processing), and reference documents cited in the other catalog files.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Document Collection
description: The document collection contains a plain text reconstructed time line for the bistatic radar experiments, a description of the amplitude, calibration process, and notes and errata from the original PDS3 archive and from the PDS3-to-PDS4 migration.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Gnc Collection
description: The GNC collection contains ASCII files of coefficients for polynomials used to adjust the amplitude of the samples from a single receiver channel. The gain calibration accounts for changes in receiver attenuator settings and the system temperature of the channel.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Index Collection
description: The index collection contains index information on each of the data files included in the original PDS3 volume set.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Prr Collection
description: The prr collection contains amplitude-corrected time samples.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Prt Collection
description: The prt collection contains amplitude-corrected time samples which have also been corrected for the major sources of Doppler shift and other frequency effects.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Sc1 Collection
description: The sc1 collection contains ASCII files of coefficients for polynomials used to adjust the frequency of the directly propagating carrier signal so that it is approximately centered in the 25000 Hz output bandwidth. Each sc1 file covers approximately 16 minutes in (nominally) one second steps and is valid for both RCP and LCP. The sc1 files were created in the 1990's and were reformatted for archive in 2008. File names have the form ydddhhmm.sc1 where 'y' and 'ddd' are the same as for prr files and 'hh' and 'mm' give the start hour and minute, respectively, for the data in each file.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Sc2 Collection
description: The sc2 collection contains ASCII files of coefficients for polynomials used to adjust the frequency of the surface echo so that it is approximately centered in the 25000 Hz output bandwidth. It is assumed that the directly propagating carrier was centered in the previous processing step, so this is a differential frequency correction. Each sc2 file covers a single Magellan orbit in (nominally) 10 second steps and is valid for both RCP and LCP. The sc2 files were created in the 1990's and were reformatted for archive in 2008. File names have the form ydddhhmm.sc2, where 'y' and 'ddd' are the same as for prr files and 'hh' and 'mm' give the start hour and minute, respectively, for the data in each file.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Spc Collection
description: The spc collection contains 10-second averages of power spectra and voltage cross-spectra derived from the samples in the prt directory. The cross-spectra are between the two received polarizations (RCP and LCP). The spc files are in ASCII format. There are three spc files -- one for each Magellan orbit.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Srf Collection
description: The srf collection contains power spectra for the RCP and LCP channels which were used to 'equalize' (flatten) the spectral response of the filters in the receiving system. Since the filtering is entirely digital, there is no reason to believe that the RCP and LCP noise power spectra would differ; but separate srf files have been derived for the two channels. Standard deviation of individual frequency bins is about 1.3 percent of the mean.

title: Magellan Bistatic Radar Calibrated: Srg Collection
description: The srg collection contains a single file summarizing the Magellan bistatic radar experiment geometry at one-minute time increments over eight hours starting at 1994-06-05T13:00:00Z.

title: Radio Science Receiver SFDU Software Interface Specification

title: Open Loop Data Interface
description: This collection contains one or more versions of the DSN Open Loop Data Interface, supporting documents, example data files. Publication year is the publication year of the first version in this collection.

title: RSC-11-10A Collection
description: This collection contains one or more versions of the DSN RSC-11-10A Software Interface Specification (SIS) used for delivering open loop data to science investigators in the 1980s. An example data file and two documents explaining extraction of values from a binary file are included in the collection.

title: DSN Radio Science System Original Data Record (ODR) RSC-11-11 Format
description: This collection contains one or more versions of the DSN RSC-11-11 Software Interface Specification (SIS) used for delivering open loop data to science investigators in the 1980 and 1998s. An example data file and two documents explaining extraction of values from a binary file are included in the collection.

title: RSC-11-6 Collection
description: This collection contains one or more versions of the DSN RSC-11-6 Software Interface Specification (SIS) used for delivering open loop data to science investigators in the early 1980s. An example data file and a document explaining extraction of values from a binary file is included in the collection.

title: TRK-2-25 DSN Archival Tracking Data File Interface
description: This collection contains several versions of the TRK-2-25 DSN Archival Tracking Data File (ATDF or TDF) interface. It also contains example TDFs from the Voyager (1979) and Cassini (2001) missions and an explanation of how the binary files can be unpacked. Publication year of this collection is the publication year of the first version in the collection.

title: Apollo 14 CPLEE Electron and Ion Count Rates and Fluxes Data Collection
description: This collection contains fixed-width ASCII tables of raw counts and derived electron and ion fluxes (40-20,000 eV) from the Apollo 14 ALSEP Charged Particle Lunar Environment Experiment (CPLEE). Two analyzers observed particles: one vertically oriented and one at 60 degrees toward lunar west. Data were acquired via automatic 19.2-second voltage stepping (15-channel spectrum) and manual 2.4-second sampling modes. The dataset includes housekeeping measurements and the necessary time and mode identification information. Coverage spans 05 February 1971 through 02 March 1973, including the major solar flare of 04 August 1972.
instruments: Apollo 14 Lunar Surface Experiments Package Charged Particle Lunar Environment Experiment
instrument_hosts: The Apollo 14 Lunar Surface Experiments Package

title: Apollo 14 CPLEE Electron and Ion Count Rates and Fluxes Document Collection
description: This collection contains documentation for the Apollo 14 CPLEE Electron and Ion Count Rates and Fluxes bundle. It includes data description document that explains the data products, CPLEE calibration, and the conversion of raw particle counts to derived fluxes.

title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mini-RF Bistatic Browse Data collection
description: This data collection contains the browse JPEG files created for each Stokes Parameter (S1-S4) and Circular Polarization Ration (CPR) derived data product. The derived data products themselves were generated from the Bistatic Mini-RF instrument flown aboard the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO).

title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mini-RF Bistatic Calibrated Data collection
description: This data collection contains the calibrated data from the Bistatic Mini-RF instrument flown aboard the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO) spacecraft. This includes the receiver time series data which has been processed into an image and the housekeeping data calibrated to engineering units.

title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mini-RF Bistatic Derived Data collection
description: This data collection contains the derived data from the Mini-RF instrument flown aboard the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). The data inclues the geometry backplanes information, Stokes Parameters (S1-S4) and the Circular Polarization Ration (CPR).

title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mini-RF Bistatic Raw Data collection
description: This data collection contains the raw data returned by the Mini-RF instrument flown aboard the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). This includes the unprocessed receiver time series, the housekeeping and state of health, and the internal calibration data products.

title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mini-RF Bistatic Document Collection
description: This documentation collection includes documentation describing the raw, calibrated, and derived data collections from the Bistatic Mini-RF instrument flown aboard the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO) spacecraft. It includes a document describing the processing done to generate some of the data products.
